dataElements.description: 1: No water conservation plan in place:No water conservation plan in place. 2: Minimal water conservation plan in place:Basic water-saving practices implemented ad hoc.. 3: Basic water conservation plan in place:Formal water management plan exists but not fully operational or enforced. 4: Moderate water conservation plan in place:Active, enforced water conservation plan reduces usage significantly during drought. 5: Full/Comprehensive water conservation plan in place:Advanced, adaptive water management strategy ensures sustainable use and rapid drought response across all facility operations.

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.6 Does the health care facility Have a plan for relocating supplies and services in case of outbreaks and epidemics that may overwhelm the facility or increase demand due to severe drought
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.6 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No plan for relocation of supplies:No relocation plan for supplies or services. 2: Minimal  plan for relocation of supplies:Informal ideas about relocating supplies exist but no formal plan. 3: Basic  plan for relocation of supplies:Basic relocation plan exists but covers only essential items. 4: Moderate  plan for relocation of supplies:Comprehensive, tested relocation plan for supplies and services during emergencies. 5: Full  plan for relocation of supplies:Fully coordinated relocation system with redundancy and pre-arranged partners, ensuring uninterrupted care even under extreme demand.

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.7 Does the health care facility Have established requirements or provide incentives to encourage water conservation in the facility and also in the communities
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.7 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No Requirements/Incentives for water conservation:No requirements or incentives for water conservation. 2: Minimal Requirements/Incentives for water conservation  Occasional messages about saving water, no formal programs.: 3: Basic Requirements/Incentives for water conservation:Requirements or incentives in place within the facility only. 4: Moderate Requirements/Incentives for water conservation:Water conservation promoted internally and actively supported in the surrounding community. 5: Full Requirements/Incentives for water conservation:Comprehensive water-saving programs integrated into both facility operations and community outreach with strong participation and measurable impacts.

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.8 Does the health care facility Have a coordinated plan with health municipal department heads to ensure appropriate preparations for ongoing drought conditions
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.8 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No coordinated drought preparation plan:No coordinated drought preparation plan with municipal health departments. 2: Minimal coordinated drought preparation plan:Informal or occasional discussions about drought risks. 3: Basic coordinated drought preparation plan:Formal planning with health municipal heads happens but is infrequent or limited. 4: Moderately coordinated drought preparation plan:Regular, coordinated drought preparedness planning with municipal health authorities. 5: Fully coordinated drought preparation plan:Highly integrated, continuous coordination with municipal leaders to maintain full drought preparedness and response readiness.

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.9 Does the health care facility Explore the relationship between social learning and adaptation measures in the face of drought threats to identify and implement the best behavioral responses from succ
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.9 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No Exploration/Learning and Adaptation Measure:No exploration or learning from other facilities’ drought adaptation. 2: Minimal Exploration/Learning and Adaptation Measure:Some awareness of successful examples but no structured learning process. 3: Basic Exploration/Learning and Adaptation Measure:Occasional benchmarking or workshops to learn about adaptation measures. 4: Moderately  Exploration/Learning and Adaptation Measure:Structured efforts to study, adopt, and adapt best practices based on social learning. 5: Fully  Exploration/Learning and Adaptation Measure  Dynamic systems in place for continuous social learning, innovation, and application of leading drought resilience behaviors and strategies.:

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.10 Does the health care facility Undertake risk assessments of the supply chain for essential medical and nonmedical products
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.10 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No Supply Chain Risk Assessment coducted:No supply chain risk assessments conducted. 2: Minimal  Supply Chain Risk Assessment coducted:Limited recognition of supply chain vulnerabilities without formal assessment. 3: Basic  Supply Chain Risk Assessment coducted:Periodic supply chain risk assessments performed for key products. 4: Moderately Supply Chain Risk Assessment coducted:Comprehensive, regular risk assessments of medical and nonmedical supply chains. 5: Full/Comprehensive  Supply Chain Risk Assessment coducted:Integrated risk management system anticipates and addresses supply chain vulnerabilities before they impact operations.

dataElements.name: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.11 Does the health care facility Have secure access to essential backup food sources via multiple agreements with different vendors and through cooperative agreements with other health care facilities
dataElements.shortName: drought-infraTTP-shcfo-4.3.11 Does the health care
dataElements.description: 1: No backup food supply agreement in place:No backup food supply agreements in place. 2: Minimal backup food supply agreement in place:Informal relationships with a few suppliers but no secure agreements. 3: Basic backup food supply agreement in place:Formal agreements with a few vendors for emergency food supply. 4: Moderate backup food supply agreement in placen:Multiple agreements with different vendors and some cooperative agreements with nearby facilities. 5: Full/Comprehensive backup food supply agreement in place:Robust, redundant food security network established, ensuring continuous access to essential food supplies through diverse, tested channels.
